title: hoya pubicorolla subsp. pubicorolla
intro: Hoya pubicorolla subsp. pubicorolla, also known as the false waxflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Hoya pubicorolla subsp. pubicorolla include its ability to aid in the treatment of wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and crushing the leaves, which can then be brewed into a tea or made into a tincture for medicinal use.
